title: recon JSON output
description: Stable v1.0 contract for `recon <domain> --json` output. Mirrors docs/schema.md; the dataclass in recon_tool.models.TenantInfo and the formatter in recon_tool.formatter.format_tenant_dict are the implementation source of truth. Consumers should ignore unknown properties — additive changes are non-breaking within 1.x. See tests/test_json_schema_contract.py for the executable contract.

tenant_id.description: Microsoft 365 tenant UUID. Null for non-M365 domains.

display_name.description: Organization display name. Falls back to the queried domain when no better signal is available.

default_domain.description: Primary domain within the tenant. Falls back to the queried domain when no tenant was detected.

queried_domain.description: The domain the user passed (after validation and lowercasing).

provider.description: One-line provider summary (e.g. 'Microsoft 365 (primary) via Proofpoint gateway + Google Workspace (secondary)').

confidence.description: Overall confidence in the merged result.

region.description: Geographic region when detectable via OIDC (e.g. 'NA', 'EU', 'WW').

google_idp_name.description: Third-party IdP name for Google Workspace when detectable.

likely_primary_email_provider.description: Inferred primary (hedged) when MX is a gateway and DKIM/TXT/OIDC points to a specific downstream.

email_gateway.description: MX-detected email security gateway (Proofpoint, Mimecast, etc.).

sources.description: Source names that successfully contributed to the result.

partial.description: True when any core source is in degraded_sources (CT-only degradation does not flip this flag).

degraded_sources.description: Source names that failed or were unavailable.

slugs.description: Stable fingerprint slugs for programmatic matching.

detection_scores.description: Per-slug detection score level aggregated from evidence.

insights.description: Derived intelligence lines. Exact wording may evolve; presence and signal types are stable.

domain_count.description: Number of domains in the tenant.

tenant_domains.description: All domains found in the tenant.

related_domains.description: Domains inferred from CNAME breadcrumbs, CT logs, or autodiscover delegation.

email_security_score.description: Count of the 5 email controls present: DMARC reject/quarantine, DKIM selectors, SPF strict (-all), MTA-STS, BIMI.

dmarc_policy.description: DMARC policy when a DMARC record is present.

site_verification_tokens.description: TXT tokens observed on the apex (e.g. 'google-site-verification=', 'MS=').

ct_provider_used.description: Which CT provider answered, or '(cached)' suffix when data came from the per-domain CT cache.

ct_subdomain_count.description: Number of subdomains returned after filtering.

ct_cache_age_days.description: Age of the CT cache entry in days when cached data was used. Null when data came from a live provider.

cert_summary.description: Certificate transparency summary. Null when no CT data was obtained.

cloud_instance.description: Microsoft cloud sovereignty from M365 OIDC discovery.

lexical_observations.description: Hedged observations from CT subdomain lexical taxonomy.

bimi_identity.description: BIMI VMC identity. Null when no BIMI VMC was observed.

slug_confidences.description: Bayesian per-slug posterior means (stable v2.0+). Each entry is a [slug, posterior] pair with posterior in [0, 1]. Populated only when --fusion is passed.

posterior_observations.description: Marginal posteriors over high-level claims from the Bayesian network at recon_tool/data/bayesian_network.yaml (v1.9; stable v2.0+). Each entry includes the posterior P(node=present | E) and the central 80% credible interval. Populated only when --fusion is passed; empty array otherwise. The Beta layer (slug_confidences) operates on raw evidence; this network layer propagates through chained claims.

surface_attributions.description: Per-subdomain attribution to a SaaS or infrastructure provider, derived from CNAME-chain classification of related_domains. Each entry maps one subdomain to its primary service. Application-tier matches (Auth0, Shopify, Zendesk, ...) take precedence over infrastructure-tier matches (Cloudflare, Fastly, CloudFront, ...) when a chain produces both — the infrastructure layer is recorded in infra_slug / infra_name as supplementary context. Empty array when no related domains classified.

evidence_conflicts.description: Top-level array of cross-source disagreements (v1.7+). Each entry names a merged field where 2+ sources provided different non-None values, with every candidate preserved. Empty array when sources agreed. The conflict-aware merger picks a winner using source-confidence ordering; this array surfaces the alternates so consumers can see what was discarded. The legacy `conflicts` dict that --explain emits remains for backwards compatibility.

chain_motifs.description: CNAME chain motifs (v1.7+). Each entry records an ordered proxy/CDN/origin shape (e.g. Cloudflare → AWS origin, Akamai → Azure origin) that fired on a related subdomain's CNAME chain. Observable structure only — never an ownership claim. Empty array when no motif matched any related subdomain. The motif catalog lives in recon_tool/data/motifs.yaml and can be extended via ~/.recon/motifs.yaml (additive only).

infrastructure_clusters.description: Per-domain CT co-occurrence community detection report (v1.8+). Always emitted. ``algorithm`` records which path produced the partition (``louvain`` for small graphs, ``connected_components`` when the graph exceeds the size cap, ``skipped`` when no graph could be built). Modularity is 0.0 in the fallback / skipped paths. Members within each cluster are sorted; clusters are sorted by size descending. Observable structure only — clusters describe co-issuance, not ownership.

fingerprint_metadata.description: Per-slug relationship metadata (v1.8+). Object keyed by detected fingerprint slug. Each value is `{product_family, parent_vendor, bimi_org}` — descriptive hints sourced from the fingerprint YAML. Slugs without any populated metadata are omitted entirely. Empty object when no detected slug carries metadata. Drives the v1.8 ecosystem hypergraph; never an ownership assertion.

unclassified_cname_chains.description: OPTIONAL — present only when ``--include-unclassified`` is passed. CNAME chains the surface classifier resolved but couldn't attribute to any cname_target fingerprint. Each entry: {subdomain, chain: [hop1, hop2, terminal]}. Wildcard-DNS echoes are filtered out before this list is populated. Feeds the fingerprint-discovery loop in validation/find_gaps.py and the /recon-fingerprint-triage Claude skill.

explanation_dag.description: Present with --explain on the lookup_tenant MCP tool. Top-level keys: evidence, slugs, rules, signals, insights — each a list of records with id and references.

wildcard_sibling_clusters.description: Stable v1.7+. Each entry is the deduplicated, sorted list of non-wildcard SANs from a single cert that also covered ≥1 wildcard SAN. Empty array when CT data showed no wildcard certs or none had concrete siblings to harvest. Bounded — at most 10 clusters, 20 names per cluster.

deployment_bursts.description: Stable v1.7+. Cohorts of cert issuances clustered by `not_before` proximity (default 60s window, min 3 distinct non-wildcard SANs). Output is intentionally relative — span_seconds + name list — and never claims ownership. Empty array when no cluster meets the minimum size. Bounded — at most 8 bursts, 25 names per burst.

window_start.description: ISO-8601 UTC of earliest not_before in this burst.

window_end.description: ISO-8601 UTC of latest not_before in this burst.

names.description: Distinct non-wildcard SANs co-issued inside this window, sorted.

source_type.description: DNS or HTTP source class (TXT, MX, CNAME, NS, SRV, CAA, SPF, HTTP).

SurfaceAttribution.description: Maps one subdomain to its primary service from CNAME chain classification. primary_* fields are the application-tier match when present, else the most specific infrastructure-tier match. infra_* fields are populated only when both an application and an infrastructure tier matched — they record the CDN or load balancer fronting the application service.

ChainMotif.description: One CNAME chain motif observed against a related subdomain. ``chain`` is the ordered subset of hops that matched the motif's markers — not the full original chain.

subdomain.description: Related subdomain whose chain produced the match.

chain.description: Matched hops in order, one per motif marker.

InfrastructureClusterReport.description: Stable v1.8+. Wraps the cluster list with the algorithm path and the underlying graph metrics so downstream tools can interpret the partition correctly.

algorithm.description: Path that produced the partition. ``connected_components`` is the deterministic fallback used when the graph exceeds the v1.8 node cap; ``skipped`` means no graph was built (no CT entries, fewer than two nodes, or no edges).

modularity.description: Newman-Girvan modularity for the Louvain partition. 0.0 in the fallback / skipped paths.

node_count.description: Distinct non-wildcard SAN hostnames observed across the cert entries.

edge_count.description: Edges in the co-occurrence graph (each edge weights co-issuance count).

EcosystemHyperedge.description: One observed multi-domain signature in a ``recon batch --json --include-ecosystem`` run (v1.8+). Surfaced as the top-level ``ecosystem_hyperedges`` array of the batch wrapper, where the per-domain entries appear under ``domains``. Not emitted in single-domain ``--json`` output.

edge_type.description: Rule that produced the edge: shared CT top issuer, shared BIMI VMC organization, shared fingerprint parent_vendor, or shared fingerprint-slug pair (≥2 overlap).

key.description: Value the rule fired on. Issuer name, BIMI org, parent vendor, or — for ``shared_slugs`` — the comma-joined intersection of overlapping slug names.

members.description: Sorted apex domains that share the signature.

FingerprintMetadata.description: Relationship hints for one fingerprint slug. Stable v1.8+. Each field is optional — at least one will be non-null when this record appears.

parent_vendor.description: Corporate parent the product rolls up to (e.g. "Microsoft", "Salesforce" for Slack).

bimi_org.description: Exact organization name expected on a BIMI VMC for domains under this product.

InfrastructureCluster.description: One community in the per-domain SAN co-occurrence graph. Stable v1.8+.

cluster_id.description: Stable id within this report (largest cluster is 0).

members.description: SAN hostnames in this cluster, sorted.

shared_cert_count.description: Number of cert-entry contributions across edges fully inside this cluster (not unique certs).

dominant_issuer.description: Most common issuer name across contributing certs.

EvidenceConflict.description: A single field where two or more sources provided different non-None values during merge. Stable v1.7+.

field.description: Merged field name. One of: display_name, auth_type, region, tenant_id, dmarc_policy, google_auth_type.

UnclassifiedCnameChain.description: A CNAME chain captured during surface classification that did not match any cname_target fingerprint. Surfaced only with ``--include-unclassified``. ``chain`` is ordered: each hop is a CNAME target, terminal last. Useful for proposing new fingerprints — the terminal hostname is usually a third-party SaaS endpoint we don't yet recognize, an intra-org CDN, or noise.

PosteriorObservation.description: Per-node Bayesian posterior with 80% credible interval (v1.9; stable v2.0+). The interval reflects evidence sparsity and cross-source conflict — wide intervals for hardened-target sparse cases, tight intervals for densely-corroborated cases. ``sparse=true`` flags the passive-observation ceiling. ``conflict_provenance`` (v1.9.1+) carries the source-pair detail behind any n_eff penalty.

interval_low.description: Lower bound of the 80% credible interval.

interval_high.description: Upper bound of the 80% credible interval.

evidence_used.description: Bound observations that fired for this node, formatted as ``slug:<name>`` or ``signal:<name>``.

n_eff.description: Effective sample size used to derive the interval; lower means wider interval.

sparse.description: True when n_eff equals the floor — interval reflects passive-observation ceiling, not strong belief.

conflict_provenance.description: Cross-source disagreements that contributed to this node's n_eff penalty (v1.9.1+). Empty array when no conflicts dampened the interval. Surfaces the source-pair detail dropped by the legacy count-only path; pairs with the existing top-level ``evidence_conflicts`` array.

NodeConflict.description: One cross-source disagreement carried through to a NodePosterior (v1.9.1+). ``field`` is the merged TenantInfo field whose sources disagreed; ``sources`` lists the distinct sources that contributed candidate values; ``magnitude`` is the n_eff penalty (in n_eff units) this conflict applied to the node.

field.description: Merged field name. One of: display_name, auth_type, region, tenant_id, dmarc_policy, google_auth_type.

sources.description: Distinct source identifiers that contributed a candidate value for this field.

magnitude.description: n_eff penalty this single conflict applied. Uniform at v1.9.1; exposed as a number so future per-node relevance weighting is schema-additive.

DeltaReport.description: Output shape of `recon delta <domain>` and `recon <domain> --compare <file>`. Stable.

BatchResult.description: Output shape of ``recon batch <file> --json``. Wraps one ``TenantInfo`` per queried domain under ``domains``, with an optional top-level ``ecosystem_hyperedges`` array emitted when ``--include-ecosystem`` is set. Stable v2.0. Per-domain entries follow the top-level ``TenantInfo`` schema in this file.

domains.description: Per-domain ``TenantInfo`` results, one entry per input apex.

ecosystem_hyperedges.description: Multi-domain signatures observed across the batch. Present only when ``--include-ecosystem`` is set; absent otherwise. Each entry is one ``EcosystemHyperedge``.
